name: Las trialeras de La Costera — Circular desde Xàtiva
region_intro: Mountain biking around Xàtiva offers diverse and rugged natural landscapes, making it a compelling destination for outdoor activities. The region features a blend of mountains, river valleys, and unique geological formations, providing varied terrain for mountain bikers. Trails navigate through areas like the Sierra del Castell and the Albaida River valley, with significant elevation changes. This network caters to a range of skill levels, from moderate to difficult. Best mountain bike trails around Xàtiva The most popular mountain bike trail is Las trialeras de La Costera — Circular desde Xàtiva, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 59 minutes to complete. This difficult route requires very good physical fitness and advanced riding skills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Bastida Refuge – Cova Negra loop from Xàtiva, a difficult 30.1 miles (48.5 km) path. This route passes through the Cova Negra area, demanding good fitness and advanced skills. Local mountain bikers also love the Gaspar Waterfall and Pool – Gorgo de la Escalera loop from Xàtiva, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail leading through scenic waterfalls and natural pools, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Mountain biking around Xàtiva is defined by its mountains, river valleys, and historical sites like Xàtiva Castle. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Xàtiva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Xàtiva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Xàtiva?
A: Xàtiva offers an extensive network of mountain bike trails, with over 60 routes available on komoot. These trails cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, winding through diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Xàtiva?
A: The most popular times for mountain biking in Xàtiva are April and September. The weather during these months is generally favorable, providing comfortable conditions for exploring the region's trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Xàtiva?
A: The terrain around Xàtiva is diverse and rugged, characterized by a blend of mountains, river valleys, and unique geological formations. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, with trails navigating areas like the Sierra del Castell and the Albaida River valley. This varied landscape provides a stunning backdrop for your rides.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Xàtiva?
A: While many trails in Xàtiva are moderate to difficult, there are still options for less experienced riders. Komoot lists 10 easy mountain bike routes in the area, perfect for those looking for a gentler introduction to the region's cycling opportunities.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Xàtiva is well-known for its challenging routes. There are 24 difficult mountain bike trails listed on komoot. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e910392192/las-trialeras-de-la-costera-circular-desde-xativa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las trialeras de La Costera — Circular desde Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; is a 43.3 km loop requiring very good physical fitness and advanced riding skills. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38207660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Bastida Refuge – Cova Negra loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt;, a 48.5 km path through the Cova Negra area.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural pools to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region boasts beautiful natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17308417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaspar Waterfall and Pool – Gorgo de la Escalera loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 49.3 km trail that leads through scenic waterfalls and natural pools. You can also visit the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt; in nearby Anna, which features a natural pool and a 25-meter waterfall.

Q: Can I visit historical sites like Xàtiva Castle while mountain biking?
A: Yes, Xàtiva's surroundings blend natural beauty with rich history. The majestic Xàtiva Castle, perched on a mountain ridge, offers breathtaking views and can be a rewarding destination or viewpoint along your ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19430674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesa Castle – Hermitage of Saint Joseph loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; is a 50.7 km route that includes historical landmarks.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: Beyond waterfalls, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5292827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellús Reservoir and spillway&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4070836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera de Anna&lt;/a&gt;. The Cova Negra Municipal Nature Park, a biodiversity hotspot and archaeological site, is also accessible and features numerous trails suitable for mountain biking.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Xàtiva?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails in Xàtiva are designed as circular or loop routes, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e910392192/las-trialeras-de-la-costera-circular-desde-xativa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las trialeras de La Costera — Circular desde Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5299021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bitter Spring – Sellent Viewpoint loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Xàtiva?
A: The mountain bike routes in Xàtiva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Xàtiva's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and challenging yet rewarding rides.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic vistas along the trails?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Xàtiva provides several excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5299021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bitter Spring – Sellent Viewpoint loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging ride that offers rewarding views from the Sellent viewpoint. Additionally, the Xàtiva Castle itself provides breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass through river valleys?
A: Yes, the region's geography includes river valleys that trails often follow or cross. The Cova Negra Municipal Nature Park, located in a narrow valley along the Albaida River, is a prime example, offering trails that contrast riverside vegetation with Mediterranean mountain flora.
tips: Old well (18th century) to collect snow that would later be turned into ice to trade or use as a preservative. It is about 10m in diameter and the same in depth. It also has beautiful views towards the city of Xàtiva.

From this viewpoint of the hermitage, which has some benches, you can contemplate fantastic views towards Játiva and its castle. Here you can take the opportunity to sit down and rest a bit on the way.

Nice path with very nice views of the castle of Xativa. From the N-340 route, the first meters are uphill with a very steep slope and then there are 200m impassable due to the fall of many pine trees (January 2022) that I hope they can remove soon. After this section, the path is clean and very entertaining, passing through the Barranco de la Font Voltada until reaching the town of Bixquert.
Very fun path with stretches of road between orange plantations. It does not present technical difficulty (easy category - blue).
Somewhat hidden path that runs to the side of the road and thus avoid asphalt for those who love mtb. It is entertaining and does not present technical difficulties
Site with incredible views. It is accessed by a hiking trail that can be done by mtb with great caution as it is very narrow and technical in sections.

Site with incredible views of the Cairent ravine. You have to be very careful to get there with mtb since it is a path that is very narrow in sections and with a loose rock surface becoming somewhat technical.

Very nice and entertaining path that runs along the section of the A-7 motorway. It does not present too much technical difficulty (easy - blue), you just have to be careful when reaching the ravine because the step is somewhat risky. The start from El Portalet is somewhat hidden and it is necessary to go down a large step behind the truck trap.
**Very nice and entertaining path that runs along the section of the A-7 motorway. It does not present too much technical difficulty (easy - blue), you just have to be careful when reaching the ravine because the step is somewhat risky. The start from El Portalet is somewhat hidden and it is necessary to go down a large step behind the truck trap.
Path without technical difficulty (easy - blue) in a very pleasant environment of trees. It allows you to get to Casa Quintana and from there connect with the hiking trail to La Cova Negra.
House built in the form of a very curious fortress. It is located a few meters from the fountain with the same name, which is accessed by a path surrounding the house.
Very entertaining path with beautiful views of the Albaida River and that allows you to reach the Les Arcades bridge and the fountain with the same name
